title: Performance Monitoring
objectives: Capture app-start, screen-render and network traces
Add custom traces around business flows
Set SLIs and alert on regressions

sections.markdown: Firebase Performance Monitoring auto-collects app-start, foreground/background, screen rendering, and network request timings. You can add custom traces to time any business flow — and see p50/p95 in the console per app version.

rows: Duration, success rate, payload size per endpoint

sections.markdown: Pick 2–3 top-line SLIs (cold start p95, checkout trace p95, home network p95). Alert when they regress by > 20% between two releases. This turns 'the app feels slow' into an SLO that ships or blocks releases.

Instrumenting everything → data overload + noise. Trace the flows that matter to users + revenue; use custom attributes to slice by network type / device tier. On top of Firebase Performance, Android Macrobenchmark and Impeller GPU frame telemetry catch different issues.

flashcards.back: App start, foreground/background, screen render (slow/frozen frames), and HTTP request timings.
Cold-start p95 or a key custom trace p95, regressing >20% between releases.

quiz.explain: Auto instrumentation focuses on performance signals.

quiz.q: Best sign that performance work is winning?
options: Cold-start / key-flow p95 improves across releases

quiz.explain: Instrument what matters; slice with attributes.

challenges.text: Turn on Performance; observe app_start and network signals.
Add a custom trace around a checkout flow with attributes.
Compare p95 across releases and gate a rollout on regression.
Correlate performance signals with Analytics events + Crashlytics for holistic release quality.

interviewQuestions: How do you add a custom trace?
Which SLIs would you alert on?
How do you compare p95 across releases?
Perf vs Macrobenchmark vs Impeller telemetry?
